findings.message: Using curl without --proto "=https" flag is security-sensitive. The HTTP client may follow redirects to clear-text HTTP protocol, which is insecure and can be sniffed, modified or corrupted by attackers.

Using curl without --proto "=https" flag is security-sensitive. The HTTP client may follow redirects to clear-text HTTP protocol, which is insecure and can be sniffed, modified or corrupted by attackers.

Using curl without --proto "=https" flag is security-sensitive. The HTTP client may follow redirects to clear-text HTTP protocol, which is insecure and can be sniffed, modified or corrupted by attackers.

Package installation without --ignore-scripts flag is security-sensitive. Package managers automatically execute shell scripts during installation, which can be exploited if a dependency is compromised. Use --ignore-scripts flag to disable automatic script execution (e.g., 'npm install --ignore-scripts').

Package installation without --ignore-scripts flag is security-sensitive. Package managers automatically execute shell scripts during installation, which can be exploited if a dependency is compromised. Use --ignore-scripts flag to disable automatic script execution (e.g., 'npm install --ignore-scripts').
Arguments in long RUN instruction should be sorted alphabetically. Found: [requests, numpy, pandas]. Expected: [numpy, pandas, requests]

Automatically installing recommended packages is security-sensitive. Recommended packages may contain vulnerabilities or unnecessary code that increases the attack surface. Use --no-install-recommends flag for apt/apt-get or --without-recommends for aptitude to avoid installing unnecessary packages.

Automatically installing recommended packages is security-sensitive. Recommended packages may contain vulnerabilities or unnecessary code that increases the attack surface. Use --no-install-recommends flag for apt/apt-get or --without-recommends for aptitude to avoid installing unnecessary packages.

Cache should be cleaned after package installation. Package manager cache should be removed to reduce Docker image size. Use 'apt-get clean && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*' for apt, 'yum clean all' for yum, 'dnf clean all' for dnf, or '--no-cache' flag for apk.

Cache should be cleaned after package installation. Package manager cache should be removed to reduce Docker image size. Use 'apt-get clean && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*' for apt, 'yum clean all' for yum, 'dnf clean all' for dnf, or '--no-cache' flag for apk.

Cache should be cleaned after package installation. Package manager cache should be removed to reduce Docker image size. Use 'apt-get clean && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*' for apt, 'yum clean all' for yum, 'dnf clean all' for dnf, or '--no-cache' flag for apk.

Consent flag should be set to avoid manual input. Package manager commands should include consent flags to enable non-interactive execution. Use '-n' or '--non-interactive' for gdebi, '-y' for apt/apt-get/yum/dnf, '--non-interactive' for zypper, or '--noconfirm' for pacman.

Disabling builder sandboxes with --security=insecure is security-sensitive. This grants full Linux capabilities to the RUN command, potentially allowing container escape and unauthorized host system access. Only use this when absolutely necessary and ensure the executed commands are fully trusted.
